name: Grade 1 Geography — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

lessons.title: Practice map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

units.title: Investigate: map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to map keys, cardinal directions, landforms, and human-environment connections.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
